comment.body:
@Trader McGraw , with all due respect, what I’m trying to explain to people is that the Greeks are overemphasized. 
Many people like to talk about them because they get to use fancy-sounding words, and it makes them seem really smart while they desperately try to explain everything/every market moves with the greeks.
Indeed, dealer flow is a very significant factor in the market, especially in the microstructure. But it’s not the only influencing factor, and it’s not always important.

Yesterday’s rally was not some "gamma/charm rally", that’s simply nonsense. 
I wrote in the weekly post, without mentioning any greeks, that funds are strategically scalping the long side and would be buying on Monday. And that’s exactly what happened. Also showed and explained how market is underpricing the real risk bcs it doesn't see whats going on (Do you even read my posts btw?)
Dealer flow is reflexive, and volatility plays a very significant role, which people can only understand by monitoring different tenors. Many analysts lack this aspect.

comment.body:
Dealer flow and even vol cycles have about 3–4 months of predictive power, no more. Every quant knows this. ...Warren Buffett knows it, and Benjamin Graham knows it too.
And let me emphasize: vol cycles! And gamma, charm, speed, and the rest can only be interpreted accurately in relation to volatility

Myself, I will never have insider friends like Alma have. But her bigger picture discussion is helping me to see a bigger picture. Will I know how the bigger picture in the future looks like without Alma. No. 
But I will probably start to read between the lines when politicians and the big boys are talking. They are almost never saying what they are really after.

comment.body:
1) You have to have metrics for that to see that market is in a rangebounding, oscillating environment.
You have to learn to read between the lines, and watch the timing of the informations.
Me personally, while I have friends from here and there and I hear rumors and facts, I like to think myself first, then ask them if i'm right.
This is one big puzzle. There’s only one or very few ways the different pieces fit together. 
I really can’t say anything else besides that you have to keep learning. I myself learn a lot, read a lot, and think even more.
Some plp read my posts back bcs there I show you how i derive things. I advise for you too. I don't hide anything.

2) Microstructure is the part of the macrostructure. Macro first, then micro. Otherwise you won't see where you are.

3) ... See answer Nr.1, and also, pls allow me to share infos with you :)) 
I'm doing what an analyst is supposed to do.
